content:
Assertion (A) : The pH of acid rain is less than 5.6.

Reason (R) : Carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere dissolves in rain water and becomes carbonic acid.

options.content: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

explanation:
Both assertion and reason are correct but reason is not the correct explanation of assertion. Normally, rain water has a pH of 5.6 due to the presence of H$$^+$$ ions formed by the reaction of rain water with carbon dioxide present in the atmosphere.

$$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(l)+\mathrm{CO}_2(g) \longrightarrow \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3(a q)$$

$$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{CO}_3(a q) \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{H}^{+}(a q)+\mathrm{HCO}_3^{-}(a q)$$

When the $$\mathrm{pH}$$ of rain water drops below 5.6, it is called acid rain.
